pattern: Treating each board layout as a graph node, a breadth-first search from the start finds the fewest tile slides to the goal, or proves it unreachable.
hints: Each board is a state; a move produces a neighbouring state, so the fewest moves is a shortest path in that state graph.
Flatten the board to a six-character string and precompute which flat positions the blank can swap with on a 2x3 grid.
Run BFS from the start string, expanding by swapping the zero with each allowed neighbour, until you reach the solved string; if the queue empties first, return -1.

edge_inputs.description: blank in a corner with a short solution

stress.description: random permutations of 0..5 reshaped into the 2x3 board, including unsolvable ones
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    perm = list(range(6))
    rng.shuffle(perm)
    board = [perm[0:3], perm[3:6]]
    return [board]
stress.note: half of all permutations are unsolvable, exercising the -1 path
